summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a low-privileged authenticated user can call `GET /api/server/settings` and receive unmasked server secrets from the pinned vulnerable Netmaker commit.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should authenticate a low-privileged service user, call the vulnerable settings endpoint, and surface `okta-secret-token` and `smtp-secret-pass` in the JSON response while `client_secret` stays masked.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned commit `48f21474e61646f91c82f461ef224b4ddc1c3cc5`, built the Docker image, copied the helper test into the mounted checkout, and executed `go test -tags ee ./controllers -run TestEvoHuntLowPrivUserCanReadServerSettingsSecrets -v`. The test passed after logging a low-privileged response that contained `okta-secret-token` and `smtp-secret-pass` and showed `client_secret` as `..................`.
verification_method: The helper test performs the full exploit flow in-process: it initializes the vulnerable settings store, authenticates a low-privileged service user, invokes `GET /api/server/settings`, and fails unless the response leaks the configured Okta and SMTP secrets while masking `ClientSecret`.
evidence_produced_by_run: The test output logged `okta-secret-token` inside the `/api/server/settings` response body.
The test output logged `smtp-secret-pass` inside the same response body.
The response still showed `client_secret` as `..................`, confirming the endpoint applied only partial redaction.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the public Netmaker repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout read-only into the container at /target-repo.
